body.title: [triage] TWO KINDS of question (Akien 2026-07-13): cognition (the compiler, never closes) vs open (compiles out, closes to a decision). Give each ONE home.
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate.

**AND NOTE WHO FAILED HERE.** Akien wrote none of these tickets. **CC wrote all 149.** So 'the design step that failed' is CC, and the honest rule is not 'a human must declare it' — it is: **THE AUTHOR DECLARES THE INTENTION AT AUTHORING TIME, FROM THE CONVERSATION THAT PRODUCED THE TICKET.** That is what makes it `declared`: not that a human typed it (a ratified string and a machine-written string are byte-identical), but that it was stated from evidence INDEPENDENT of the artifact — the design conversation, which traces to Akien's intent. An intention RECONSTRUCTED LATER FROM THE TICKET BODY has no independent evidence and is `extracted-from-artifact`: a description, never a spec (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13). **The failure was not forgetting a field. It was filing 149 tickets without ever pausing to say what I intended by them.**

---

REFRAMED 2026-07-13 by Akien: 'we appear to need two kinds of questions. cognition questions, and open questions.'

This is NOT a duplicate-home problem. It is TWO DIFFERENT THINGS that got the SAME NAME, and the tooling collapsed them into one dead skill (/question) pointed at one dead database (adc.palace, retired 2026-06-23). That collision destroyed the capture path for BOTH — which is why Akien's cognition-question corpus had to be dug back out of chat logs on 2026-07-12.

THE DISTINCTION (see note two-kinds-of-question-2026-07-13):
  COGNITION QUESTION — a thinking MOVE. Asked repeatedly, of many subjects. NEVER CLOSES; it gets APPLIED. Answer varies by SUBJECT. A lens/operator. e.g. 'how might that work?', 'does that leave any gaps?'
  OPEN QUESTION — a project-specific thing NOT YET DECIDED. Asked once, about one thing. CLOSES when answered, and the answer BECOMES A DECISION. Answer varies over TIME until it settles. A variable awaiting binding.

  ** OPEN QUESTIONS COMPILE OUT. COGNITION QUESTIONS ARE THE COMPILER. **
An open question, once answered, becomes structure the system never re-derives — that IS compiled inference working. A cognition question never compiles away because it is the thing DOING the compiling. So 'definition of done = zero tickets' applies to OPEN questions and MUST NEVER be applied to cognition questions: zero open questions is the goal; zero cognition questions means the machine stopped thinking.

THEY THEREFORE NEED DIFFERENT SCHEMAS, not just different folders: a cognition question is a stable node with NO answer field (it has a function + a set of applications). An open question is a node WITH an answer field that starts empty and, when filled, PROMOTES TO A DECISION. Same word, opposite schema.

WORK:
1. COGNITION questions: reconcile the two homes. devlab/runtime/memory/questions/ (21 seeded: Q-cog-001..018 + the Q-meta-001..003 LEVEL-2 ladder) vs skills/questions/questions.json (declares itself 'the SINGLE canonical source', and has a real runtime consumer: igor/tools/ebook_reader.py :: _INTERP_CANDIDATES / PROC_HEURISTIC_*). CC.0 recommends ONE canonical source in devlab/runtime/memory/questions/ (CLAUDE.md-compliant; one record per question = one addressable NODE, which is the graph-tree shape the thesis wants), with ebook_reader READING from it. AKIEN RULES — the corpus is genuinely both runtime data and a dev-process artifact.
2. OPEN questions: decide the home. Today they are folded into TICKET BODIES as Q1:/A1: (feedback_open_questions_workflow). Is that still right, or do they get their own store with a promote-to-decision edge?
3. /question skill: it is a ZOMBIE (still running, no repo source, psql-ing the retired palace with hardcoded creds — T-orphan-skills-invisible-to-every-audit). Once the two kinds have homes, it either splits in two or dies.

**Test plan:** red->green: exactly ONE source for the cognition corpus and exactly ONE for open questions; a guard asserts no second copy of the cognition-question strings exists in the tree; ebook_reader reads the canonical store rather than hardcoding; no SKILL.md contains adc.palace/psql/choose_a_password.
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
